text: "A mixt multitude."... These were people that came with them out of Egypt, who were not of the race of Israel; who, by their murmuring, drew also the children of Israel to murmur: this should teach us the danger of associating ourselves with the children of Egypt; that is, with the lovers and admirers of this wicked world.
"Bdellium. Bdellium,"... according to Pliny, (lib. xxi. c. 9,) was of the color of a man’s nail, white and bright.
"Seventy men."... This was the first institution of the council or senate, called the Sanhedrim, consisting of seventy, or seventy-two senators or counselors.
"The graves of lust."... Or the sepulchres of concupiscience: so called from their irregular desire of flesh. In Hebrew Kibroth Hattavah.
